title: Components of COVID-19 vaccines authorized by Health Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## Components of COVID-19 vaccines authorized by Health Canada
Download in PDF format 112 KB, 3 pages)
Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada
Published: 2021-05-07
Known to be a potential allergen
Messenger RNA (mRNA)
Nucleoside-modified mRNA encoding the viral spike (S) glycoprotein of SARS-CoV-2
Active ingredient: Genetic code that provides the blueprint for our body's cells to make the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein in the cytoplasm, which is then displayed on the cell surface and elicits an immune response
- Genetic code that provides the blueprint for our body's cells to make the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein in the cytoplasm, which is then displayed on the cell surface and elicits an immune response
Components of the lipid nanoparticle that delivers the mRNA into the cell
ALC-0159 = 2[(polyethylene glycol)-2000]-N,N-ditetradecylacetamide
Forms a protective layer that stabilizes the nanoparticle, improves storage stability and reduces non-specific binding to proteins
- Forms a protective layer that stabilizes the nanoparticle, improves storage stability and reduces non-specific binding to proteins
Yes: Polyethylene glycol (PEG) can be an allergen
1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (DSPC)
Part of the double layer of lipids (lipid bilayer) that forms the nanoparticle
- Part of the double layer of lipids (lipid bilayer) that forms the nanoparticle
Provides structural support for the lipid bilayer of the nanoparticle and supports mobility of lipid components
- Provides structural support for the lipid bilayer of the nanoparticle and supports mobility of lipid components
ALC-0315 = (4-hydroxybutyl) azanediyl)bis(hexane-6,1-diyl)bis(2-hexyldecanoate)
Main ingredient in the lipid-nanoparticle that delivers the mRNA into the cell Cationic (positively charged) lipid that during the manufacturing process, promotes the nanoparticle to assemble into a virus size particle with the negatively charged mRNA in the middle, and facilitates mRNA
                            release from the nanoparticle once inside the cell
- Main ingredient in the lipid-nanoparticle that delivers the mRNA into the cell
- Cationic (positively charged) lipid that during the manufacturing process, promotes the nanoparticle to assemble into a virus size particle with the negatively charged mRNA in the middle, and facilitates mRNA
                            release from the nanoparticle once inside the cell
Additional ingredients
Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Monobasic potassium phosphate
Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Dibasic sodium phosphate dehydrate
Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Sugar to protect and stabilize the vaccine during freezing and prevent the particles from sticking together
- Sugar to protect and stabilize the vaccine during freezing and prevent the particles from sticking together
Normal saline (provided separately from the vaccine)
Water and salt solution added as the diluent to the vaccine
- Water and salt solution added as the diluent to the vaccine
Notes: Does not contain latex in the stopper
Known to be a potential allergen
Messenger RNA (mRNA)
Nucleoside-modified mRNA encoding the viral spike (S) glycoprotein of SARS-CoV-2
Active ingredient: Genetic code that provides the blueprint for our body's cells to make the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein in the cytoplasm, which is then displayed on the cell surface and elicits an immune response
- Genetic code that provides the blueprint for our body's cells to make the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein in the cytoplasm, which is then displayed on the cell surface and elicits an immune response
Components of the lipid nanoparticle that delivers the mRNA into the cell
Polyethylene glycol (PEG) 2000 DMG
Serves to stabilize and prolong the lifespan of the nanoparticle
- Serves to stabilize and prolong the lifespan of the nanoparticle
Yes: Polyethylene glycol (PEG) can be an allergen
1,2-distearoyl-sn-glycero-3-phosphocholine (DSPC)
Part of the double layer of lipids (lipid bilayer) that forms the nanoparticle
- Part of the double layer of lipids (lipid bilayer) that forms the nanoparticle
Provides structural support for the lipid bilayer of the nanoparticle and supports mobility of lipid components
- Provides structural support for the lipid bilayer of the nanoparticle and supports mobility of lipid components
Ionizable lipid that interacts with mRNA During the manufacturing process, promotes the nanoparticle to assemble into a virus size particle with the negatively charged mRNA in the middle and facilitates mRNA release from the nanoparticle once in the
                            cell
- Ionizable lipid that interacts with mRNA
- During the manufacturing process, promotes the nanoparticle to assemble into a virus size particle with the negatively charged mRNA in the middle and facilitates mRNA release from the nanoparticle once in the
                            cell
Additional ingredients
Base that forms a buffer and works to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Base that forms a buffer and works to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Yes: tromethamine can be a very rare cause of allergic reactions
Tromethamine hydrochloride
Salt that forms a buffer and works to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Salt that forms a buffer and works to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Yes: tromethamine can be a very rare cause of allergic reactions
Acid that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Acid that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine Helps to stabilize the particle
- Salt that forms a buffer to balance the pH (acidity) of the vaccine
- Helps to stabilize the particle
Sugar to protect and stabilize the vaccine during freezing and prevent the particles from sticking together
- Sugar to protect and stabilize the vaccine during freezing and prevent the particles from sticking together
Note: Does not contain latex in the stopper
Known to be a potential allergen
Sources of genetic code for spike protein
Modified adenovirus (ChAdOx1) containing spike protein genetic code
Active ingredient: Adenovirus that has been genetically modified to not replicate and to carry the gene for the spike protein of the SARS-CoV-2 virus Delivers genetic code of the spike protein to the nucleus of the body's cell where mRNA is made and transported to the cytoplasm of the body's cell where the spike protein is then made, displayed on the cell
                            surface and elicits an immune response
- Adenovirus that has been genetically modified to not replicate and to carry the gene for the spike protein of the SARS-CoV-2 virus
- Delivers genetic code of the spike protein to the nucleus of the body's cell where mRNA is made and transported to the cytoplasm of the body's cell where the spike protein is then made, displayed on the cell
                            surface and elicits an immune response
Additional ingredients
Buffering agent: pH (acidity) control and stability during storage
- Buffering agent: pH (acidity) control and stability during storage
L-Histidine hydrochloride monohydrate
Buffering agent: pH (acidity) control and stability during storage
- Buffering agent: pH (acidity) control and stability during storage
Magnesium chloride hexahydrate
Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (electrostatic interactions)
- Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (electrostatic interactions)
Surfactant: stabilizes the adenovirus by reducing virus sticking to surfaces and minimizing interactions where the liquid vaccine comes into contact with air
- Surfactant: stabilizes the adenovirus by reducing virus sticking to surfaces and minimizing interactions where the liquid vaccine comes into contact with air
Yes: polysorbate 80 can be a very rare cause of allergic reactions
Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (prevents free-radical induced oxidation of the adenovirus)
- Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (prevents free-radical induced oxidation of the adenovirus)
Cryo-protectant: stabilizes the adenovirus during freezing and thawing, enhances the adenovirus' ability to withstand temperature changes, and acts as a tonicity agent
- Cryo-protectant: stabilizes the adenovirus during freezing and thawing, enhances the adenovirus' ability to withstand temperature changes, and acts as a tonicity agent
Cryo-protectant: stabilizes the adenovirus during freezing and thawing and acts as a tonicity agent
- Cryo-protectant: stabilizes the adenovirus during freezing and thawing and acts as a tonicity agent
Disodium edetate dihydrate (EDTA)
Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (prevents free-radical induced oxidation of the adenovirus)
- Stabilizer: protects the vaccine from adverse conditions (prevents free-radical induced oxidation of the adenovirus)
Liquid in the vaccine vial
- Liquid in the vaccine vial
Note: Does not contain latex in the stopper
